ref: If you are on the roof of your house, don't go inside to get anything.
text: Fo …îbono fo bo n≈ãma≈ãman g…õnen owi …îbono m…î, sel…õ. G…õ≈ã sa foÍûå kii g…õmara ab…õ…õ foÍûå loo obu d…î y…õ…õ fo laa kpaa puru ilaa iko.
translated: The best of the people are those who, because they know the way to achieve success, must work hard and remain determined.

ref: ‚ÄúHere is my chosen servant! I love him, and he pleases me. I will give him my Spirit, and he will bring justice to the nations.
text: ‚ÄúF…õÍûå ker…õ …îbono n…õ l…õ…õ gibaa yii y…õ…õ …îÍûå w…îra me-g…õsun m…î. M…î-ilaa i bo me-gisen d…î; …î tansi …î kya gyi me-ginsi. Nan y…õg…õ me-Oduduu m…î …îÍûå kyena m…î d…î, de …îÍûå t…îg…õ sa g…õsinkpan so asa kp…õi-kp…õi y…õ…õ nan gyi m…îm…î p…õwu nb…õl…õ g…õsintin …îkpa so.
translated: He is a man who is not afraid of anything, and he is the one called the hunter of the forest. He is the one who kills the lion; he is the one who cuts the elephant's tusk. He is the one called Oduduwa, the one who is the king, and he is the one who brings people together so that they can live in peace and harmony.

ref: It is like what happens when a man goes away for a while and places his servants in charge of everything. He tells each of them what to do, and he orders the guard to keep alert.
text: ‚ÄúI laa w…îra f…õ…õ gikpal…õ gibono nan baa da faa. ∆Übel…õns…õ …îko ne n kyena, …î kya laar…õ oÍûå kpe …îkpa. G…õkaabono …î laa koso g…õwi m…î, o kyu m…î-g…õsun w…îra m…î-adega abaa d…î, m…îm…î …îkamaas…õ m…î‚Äër…õ m…î-l…õ…õ g…õsun w…îras…õ. ∆Ü t…îg…õ sa …îbono …î t…õ …î kya dii m…î-g…õbunono m…î y…õ…õ …î kya kyon faa, …îÍûå ker…õ d…î…îdan; …î m…õ≈ãÍûå sa …îÍûå d…õ ginsi gid…õ.‚Äù
translated: ‚ÄúI will do well to speak about this gossip now. He/She will carry the burden of my eyes, cutting off his/her head. G…õkaabono will go to the market with the goods, buy the goods to set their price, then sell them at that price. He/She will take the people and say, he/she will carry my burdens and my words well, he/she will speak the truth; then he/she will go to the people of the earth.‚Äù

ref: I was frightened and went out and hid your money in the ground. Here is every single coin!‚Äù
text: Im…îso n…õ sel…õ fo gifuu, ne n…õ kyu fo-sika k…îk…î…î m…î kyu kpaa ≈ãara …îb…î d…î. Ker…õ, fo-sika k…îk…î…î m…î ne.‚Äô
translated: Im…îso and you exchange gifts, and with your hands, you take care of the children here. Therefore, with your hands, you are here.

ref: John also told the people, ‚ÄúSomeone more powerful is going to come. And I am not good enough even to stoop down and untie his sandals.
text: Gy…în ilaa t…îg…õs…õ d…î m…î, …î kya t…îg…õ …î kya sa asa y…õ…õ, ‚ÄúMe-g…õmara m…î, …îko laa ba. ∆Ü don me ilaa kamaas…õ d…î. F…õ…õ nÍûå ba g…õs…õ de nÍûå kyu m…î-ayaawol…õ tol…õ m…î-ayaa d…î sa m…î gbaa m…î, me≈ã fo.
translated: Gy…în, the speaker, said, "I am tired, he went away. He told me and my people to gather. But my people say that until my ancestors and descendants take me, I am not going."
